description: Will the Islamic Republic of Iran's ruling regime fall by June 30, 2026? Part of a temporal cluster with March, April, and end-of-year variants. The June variant has $23.2M volume and 16.5% implied probability. Resolution requires dissolution of core structures (Supreme Leader, Guardian Council, IRGC under clerical authority), not just leadership changes.
question: Will the Iranian regime fall by June 30?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if the Islamic Republic of Iran ceases to govern by June 30, 2026. Requires dissolution, incapacitation, or replacement of core structures (Supreme Leader, Guardian Council, IRGC under clerical authority) — confirmed by broad consensus of credible reporting.

triggers.description: Core structures of the Islamic Republic are dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system, OR the regime loses de facto power over a majority of Iran's population
qualifying_scenarios: Civil war resulting in regime loss of control
Military coup replacing Islamic Republic system

exclusions: Elections, reforms, or leadership succession within the existing system
Internal coups or power shifts that PRESERVE the Islamic Republic's core structures
Partial loss of territory unless the regime no longer administers the majority of Iran's population
Challenges from rebel or exile groups unless the above majority threshold is met
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: The bar is intentionally high — this requires a 'clear break in continuity' (new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution). A scenario where Mojtaba Khamenei is replaced as Supreme Leader but the Guardian Council and IRGC remain intact would NOT qualify. The 'majority of population' test means that even significant territorial losses in peripheral regions wouldn't resolve Yes if Tehran and major cities remain under regime control.

catalyst_dates.event: March variant resolves (currently 0.15% Yes) — will establish term structure
US military strikes on Iran — regime survival market at 83.5% Yes

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: Kalshi's closest equivalent is 'Will Iran become a democracy in 2026?' (KXIRANDEMOCRACY) — a much narrower question. Regime fall on Polymarket doesn't require democracy, just end of the Islamic Republic system. A military junta replacing the mullahs would qualify on Polymarket but not Kalshi. Significant structural divergence — same geopolitical event, very different resolution criteria.

related_markets.notes: Iranian regime fall by March 31 — about to resolve No (0.15%), establishes the near-term probability floor
Iranian regime fall by April 30 — 6.5% Yes, $13.3M volume. Temporal step between March and June
Iranian regime fall before 2027 — 33.5% Yes, $12.3M volume. Year-end variant
Will the Iranian regime SURVIVE US military strikes? — 83.5% Yes. Directly measures regime resilience conditional on US action
US forces enter Iran by April 30 — 65.5% Yes. Ground entry would be the most likely catalyst for regime fall
